    Home › Manufacturers › North American Aviation

    North American Aviation

    United States · Founded 1928

    The Mustang, the X-15, and a Mach 3 bomber made of steel.

    Aircraft here1
    Longest56.62 m
    Combined built2
    First flights1964–1964
    The company

    The story so far

    North American built some of the most extraordinary aircraft of the twentieth century: the P-51 Mustang, the F-86 Sabre, and the X-15 rocket plane that still holds the speed record for a crewed aircraft.

    Its XB-70 Valkyrie was the most ambitious of all — a 56-metre bomber cruising at Mach 3, its wingtips folding down in flight to ride its own shockwave. Two were built, one survives, and nothing that large has ever flown that fast since.

    The line-up

    North American Aviation aircraft

    Silhouettes are sized by real wingspan — bigger aircraft draw bigger.

    North American XB-70 Valkyrie

    North American · Preserved — 1 of 2 survives

    Height9.14 m
    MTOW246,365 kg

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
